response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong distributed systems expertise, cloud infrastructure knowledge, and excellent software engineering practices, but falls significantly short on the minimum 8-year C++ requirement with only ~6 months of recent professional experience. Additionally, explicit technical leadership and mentoring experience is limited despite strong seniority level. The fit depends on whether C++ expertise can be developed or is truly non-negotiable for the role.
strengths: Proven expertise in large-scale distributed systems and infrastructure (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second; microservices architecture experience)
Strong cloud and infrastructure knowledge including Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, and demonstrated ability to establish engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Extensive full-stack engineering background across 19+ years with multiple shipped products, showing ability to own and deliver complex projects
concerns: Fails to meet the minimum 8-year C++ requirement—only ~6 months recent professional C++ experience (2014-2015) with no substantial C++ work in recent roles

Limited evidence of formal technical leadership or explicit mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers; current 'Staff Software Engineer' title is not equivalent to the 'Technical Lead' role with stated mentoring responsibilities
Cloud architecture experience appears infrastructure and tooling-focused rather than owning large-scale production cloud platform strategy and long-term architectural decisions

key_factors.factor: Explicit technical leadership and mentoring track record
